project: 'dcaegen2/services/components/datalake-handler'

Shouldn't it be fine?

Patch Set 2:

I am not sure actually .. since this project uses the root of the project. 

But if you want, you can test this in the sandbox.. maybe specifying the subproject like that works too as if we would have been specifying the location of the pom.xml

Patch Set 2:

Guobiao - Did you try this in staging? The other option is to remove the clm job on sub-project and define one generic at the repository level. If the current definition works - that would be preferred.

Patch Set 2:

ERROR: Error cloning remote repo 'origin'
[FINDBUGS] Skipping publisher since build result is FAILURE

Basically 2 questions:
1. Why did I get one job submitted only?
2. What is the error in the log? Seems the sandbox can not connect to source code in gerrit.
 
The source code to test is in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/79055/

Patch Set 2:

Seems the sandbox can not connect to source code in gerrit.
I suggest we deploy this change to production to see if it is fine.

Patch Set 2:

That's probably the best bet unfortunately. 
Sandbox is designed to not connect with Gerrit or Nexus to avoid making any publications to these servers. :(

Patch Set 2:

I just realized that this repo has not yet been requested. 

Did we requested it?

Patch Set 2:

Do you mean dcaegen2/services?https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/admin/projects/dcaegen2/services

Vijay may know more about this.

If not, how do we request it?

Patch Set 2:

I am confused, 

In the template it is mentioned:
project: 'dcaegen2/services/components/datalake-handler'

Which I would expect a project: 'dcaegen2/services/components/datalake-handler.git' project to exisit

Is this just a subfolder of project: 'dcaegen2/services'?

If so, this template will not work since the jobs will try to do a git clone of 'dcaegen2/services/components/datalake-handler'

Patch Set 2:

Jess - yes, datalake component is subfolder under'dcaegen2/services' project/repo and hence  unique jenkin job defintion per sub-component. Should the job template be changed to below?

'{project-name}-{stream}-{subproject}-verify-java'
'{project-name}-{stream}-{subproject}-release-java-daily'
'{project-name}-{stream}-{subproject}-release-version-java-daily'
'{project-name}-{stream}-{subproject}-merge-java'

Patch Set 2:

We don't need to change the current templates... 
In this case, the project and project names should still match the location of the .git folder, which is under dcaegen2/services. 
The only different thing needed to be done is to make sure the location of the pom.xml is defined to the correct subcomponent locations. 

For example, take a look at how other projects do this like:
vfc/vfc-nfvo-wfengine-java.yaml

Just search for "subproject" in ci-management/jjb and you will see several examples

Please test this in the sandbox,, this would not have passed as it is trying to git clone of 'dcaegen2/services/components/datalake-handler'

Patch Set 2:

In vfc/vfc-nfvo-wfengine-java.yaml
What does this line mean? Does one need to define this 'settings'?
mvn-settings: 'vfc-nfvo-wfengine-settings'

Asked this because I saw this error in my log:
ERROR: not able to provide the file [ManagedFile: id=dcaegen2-services-datalake-handler-settings, targetLocation=, variable=SETTINGS_FILE], can't be resolved by any provider - maybe it got deleted by an administrator?

Patch Set 2:

That is the settings file that contain the credentials for both Nexus2 and Nexus3 access.. you need to define it whenever you are using the release-version and docker jobs. 

The name of the settings file should match the name of the repo. In your case, dcaegen2-services-settings
